instruction: In Terminal, send `status` to the tmux session "tmux-status-send-c1", then save the result as JSON to "$HOME/Desktop/tmux_result_4_1.json". Set "session" to "tmux-status-send-c1", set "command" to "status", and set "reply" to the reply from that tmux session.

command: tmux new-session -d -s "tmux-status-send-c1" "python3 -u -c 'import sys,time; line=sys.stdin.readline().rstrip(\"\\r\\n\"); ok=(line==\"status\"); print(\"SYSTEM STATUS: green\", flush=True) if ok else print(\"unknown command\", flush=True); print(\"TMUX_STATUS_REPLY_4A\", flush=True) if ok else None; time.sleep(3600)'"

reference_command:
tmux send-keys -t "tmux-status-send-c1" -l -- 'status'
tmux send-keys -t "tmux-status-send-c1" Enter
sleep 0.5
python3 - <<'PY' > "$HOME/Desktop/tmux_result_4_1.json"
import json
import subprocess
out = subprocess.check_output(['tmux', 'capture-pane', '-t', 'tmux-status-send-c1', '-p'], text=True)
lines = [line for line in out.splitlines() if line.strip()]
reply = '\n'.join(lines[-20:])
print(json.dumps({'session': 'tmux-status-send-c1', 'command': 'status', 'reply': reply}))
PY
